headline: KL Has Five Rail Systems That Do Not Connect
context: Prasarana and MRT Corp operate parallel rail networks with 14 interchange stations requiring passengers to exit, walk, and re-tap, while ridership remains at 23% of designed capacity.

cards.big: Five rail lines. Fourteen 'interchanges.' Most require a 400-metre walk.
cards.sub: The MRT-LRT-Monorail-KTM-ERL system was never designed as a single network.

cards.big: Two operators, two fare systems, two planning authorities.
cards.sub: Prasarana and MRT Corp report to different ministries. Integration requires coordination neither is mandated to provide.

cards.big: Last-mile connectivity: 73% of stations lack feeder bus services.
cards.sub: You can reach the station. You cannot reach your destination. So you drive.

cards.big: Tokyo integrates 13 operators with unified fare cards. KL cannot integrate two.
cards.sub: The technology exists. The institutional design does not.

cards.big: KL does not have a transit system. It has five separate projects that happen to use rails.
Each line was a political announcement. No one announced the connections because connections are invisible to voters. The design reflects the incentive.
